title: Object 17
wikitext:
__NOTOC__{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 17}}

[[File:24hoursispossible.png|600px|link=]]


I was in [[Level 213]] with a friend of mine who had returned from a brief expedition. He went through a few levels, expecting to find something interesting or even just a single can of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or a growth of [[Object 10|Backshrooms]]. However, all he found was this Rubik's Cube, which he found on his way to our meetup point in the level. It did pique his interest a bit since he's been in the Backrooms for 3 years now and this is the first time he saw a Rubik's Cube in these boundless expanses, and he was happy that he found something which he can toy around even if he had no clue how to solve it. Before he would start playing with it though, he wanted to show it to me and give him some advice on how to solve it. We both grabbed a quick snack from the diner, and we sat down at this table where he would toy with the Rubiks Cube and talk about it with me. He would say about how he's finally excited to have some form of entertainment in the Backrooms, but knowing he had no idea how to solve it, I couldn't see such expression on his face. He continued to toy with it further, leaving his meal untouched, with mine almost finished. He was almost getting one side of the Rubiks Cube finished, and he was happy about it. I found that neat, but I still had to focus on finishing my meal. I looked to my plate and as I was getting the last bouts of food I had left, I heard a massive bang-like sound, and my surroundings were splattered with blood all over. I looked up, and I saw a horrifying scene which I'll never forget - it seems like the Rubiks Cube hit my friend in the face with the force of a bullet. It left a gory scene leaving my poor pal unrecognizable, and I could see deep in the face the Rubik's Cube - deformed and wiggling a bit, like it had a sentience on it's own. It then disappeared, leaving a hole on the other side of the head.


=Object 17: "'''Lubik's Cube'''"= ---- {{Simple Image Block|image=24hoursisimpossible.png|width=420px|caption=The most infamous image of a '''Lubik's Cube'''.|align=center}} ==Description== The '''Lubik's Cube''', designated as Object 17, resembles a Rubik's Cube with minor differences, like mild difficulty turning the pieces, slightly washed-out colors, and various anomalous properties that may even determine the fate of those who toy with it. Once a wanderer touches a '''Lubik's Cube''', its colors will wash out, the cube will turn cold despite the surrounding temperature, and the cube's pieces can no longer be turned by other wanderers. The cube can teleport to any nearby position at random, but usually does so when other wanderers force the pieces once another wanderer already "owns" it or when wanderers try to damage the cube. If the owner has been solving the cube for at least one minute straight, the cube's colors may randomly shift into different positions, usually reversing all solving progress. The wanderer who "owns" the '''Lubik's Cube''' will be unable to leave their current level and will be strongly compelled to solve the cube, often giving it their complete focus and ignoring other errands. Owners who encounter any complication regarding the cube, such as the cube teleporting, the cube's colors switching positions, or trying to find solutions to solve it with no avail, will often incur a short burst of anger. The outrage may lead to the user damaging the cube, and when it sustains enough damage, it may deliver detrimental or even fatal outcomes for the owner. After thirty to sixty minutes after a wanderer claims the '''Lubik's Cube''', it will suddenly vanish, and the user will be able to exit their current level. Strangely, their chances of encountering and interacting with another '''Lubik's Cube''' increase, with their subsequent cube encounter within 2-24 months. Knowing about the cube also slightly increases one's chances of encountering it, with roughly 60-80% of '''Lubik's Cubes''' encountered by those who knew it beforehand, with many seeing a potential link to the [[The Kauer Thesis|Kauer Thesis]]. So far, since its discovery in 1982, less than a thousand cubes have been discovered, with the rate of manifestation increasing over time. Once the owner solves one side of the '''Lubik's Cube''', it will assign a random outcome. The color of the solved side determines the ramification and its magnitude. When the "owner" solves one side and meets their fate, the Cube will disappear and the "owner" will not encounter another cube for 45-75 days. The cube then vanishes shortly after the fate has been granted. If two sides are solved, the wanderer will experience two fates simultaneously, one from each color, if feasible. In the extremely rare scenario where someone manages to solve a side on a '''Lubik's Cube''' without ever having touched it (example being with chopsticks), the cube will simply scramble itself again, and will not provide an outcome unless a wanderer touches, and "owns" it before solving a side. Some colors have fatal ramifications, including ones where the user's death will be of suffering. Inversely, some outcomes may grant bountiful blessings, incentivizing wanderers to play with the cube despite the cost. Fortunately, listed below are the consequences of each color, so one may be cautious when encountering and solving a '''Lubik's Cube'''. ==Outcomes of Each Color==
Note that the outcomes listed below are the eight most common ones for each color. Each color may bring about many others which are either rarer or still await discovery. {{Accordion | animation-duration = 500 | accordion-1-title = Red Side | accordion-1-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of a wanderer after they were granted the Collision fate.|file=I-aint-showin-that.png|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the red side of the cube, they will be subject to the worst ramifications, bringing about instant death at the most merciful. These outcomes may also be given if the wanderer tries to damage or dispose of the cube. *'''Skeletal Demanifestation''': All 206 of the wanderer's bones will vanish, transforming the wanderer into a slimy mass. *'''Collision''': The cube will lunge towards the user at approximately 100 kilometers per hour, often destroying much of the head and splattering its surroundings with blood, flesh, and bone remnants. *'''Fatal Transportation''': The wanderer will noclip into some of the most dangerous Backrooms levels, including most Class 5 or even Deadzone levels. *'''Frog's Demise''': The wanderer's body temperatures will gradually increase until reaching fatal levels in two minutes. *'''Liquid Pain''': A large amount of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] will spontaneously manifest above the wanderer. *'''Half Noclip''': The wanderer's legs or lower body will noclip, with the rest of the body following suit after two minutes. The wanderer then ends up in [[The Void]]. *'''Fire''': The wanderer's entire body will be set on fire. The fire will not spread to its surroundings. *'''Blessing?''': The wanderer collapses and dies on the spot.
| accordion-2-title = Orange Side | accordion-2-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of [[Level 188.8]], a level which Hazardous Transporation might send one to.|file=Whywebp.webp|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the orange side of the cube, they will be subject to deadly and painful yet survivable outcomes. These outcomes may also be given if the wanderer tries to examine or study the cube's inner workings. *'''Hazardous Transportation''': The wanderer will noclip into a dangerous level, usually a Class 4 or 5 level. *'''Blindness''': The user will go permanently blind, but can still be cured. *'''Hydrolitis Plague''': The wanderer contracts the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. *'''Freak Accident''': Within 3 weeks, the wanderer will die of a freak accident. The wanderer will be given premonitions, granting them an opportunity to subvert their fate. *'''Trapped''': The wanderer will forever be trapped on the level they currently in. *'''High Value Target''': Nearby hostile entities will prioritize eliminating the wanderer for as long as they are alive. *'''Sleep Paralysis''': Sleep paralysis will forever assail the wanderer. *'''Profoundly Deaf''': The wanderer will become profoundly deaf.
| accordion-3-title = Yellow Side | accordion-3-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=Skin allergies of a wanderer with the Almonds of Agony fate after being in a level with vapor [[Object 1|Almond Water]].|file=Evenmorewebp.webp|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the yellow side of the cube, they will experience mildly dangerous outcomes. *'''Depression''': The wanderer will experience long-lasting depression. *'''More Pain''': The pain receptors of the wanderer will become slightly more sensitive. *'''Lulled''': The wanderer will fall asleep for at least twelve hours. *'''Bad Luck''': More unfortunate events and circumstances will assail the wanderer. This includes difficulty exiting levels, lower chances entering safer levels, more entity encounters, difficulties evading entities, et cetera. *'''Obscurity''': For a year, [[Phenomenon 5]] will randomly blind the wanderer. *'''Almonds of Agony''': The user will incur a nut allergy, rendering [[Object 1|Almond Water]] dangerous. *'''Hunger''': The user will go hungry and thirsty more often. *'''Negativity''': For a few days, the wanderer's mind will be filled with negative thoughts.
| accordion-4-title = Blue Side | accordion-4-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of [[Level 238]], a level which Transportation might send one to.|file=Finallysomethingnormal.jpg|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the blue side of the cube, outcomes will be neutral with little downsides or benefits. *'''Transportation''': The user will noclip into mildly dangerous levels, usually Class 1 to 3 levels. *'''Growth Spurt''': The user will grow 1 inch (2.54 cm) in height. *'''Aging''': In 1-2 minutes, the wanderer's physical features will age five to ten years ahead. *'''Water''': A large amount of water will spontaneously manifest above the wanderer. *'''Backshrooms''': Two to five edible Backshrooms will manifest in front of the wanderer. *'''Memory Wipe''': The wanderer's short term memories, including that of the Cube itself, will be wiped clean. *'''Color blind''': The wander will instantly become color blind, most commonly Protanopia color blindness (Unable to see red, and will predominantly see blues and golds). *'''Nothing''': Nothing will happen, and the Cube will instantly disappear.
| accordion-5-title = Green Side | accordion-5-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of an M1911 pistol granted by the Self-Defense fate.|file=EM1911BABYYY.webp|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the green side of the cube, outcomes will be beneficial to the wanderer in contrast to most other colors which are negative. *'''Almond Water''': 1-3 cans of Almond Water will manifest in front of the wanderer. *'''Self-Defense''': A loaded pistol will manifest in front of the wanderer. *'''Survival Guide''': A Backrooms survival guide book will manifest in front of the wanderer. *'''Good Luck''': More auspicious events and circumstances will bless the wanderer. Usually delivers the opposite of its yellow-sided counterpart, Bad Luck. *'''Positivity''': For the next few days, the wanderer's mind will be filled with positive thoughts. The opposite effect of its yellow-side counterpart, Negativity *'''Liminal Polyglot''': The wanderer will become fluent in speaking [[Aurielleno]] and [[Jinyi]]. *'''Circadian Endurance''': The wanderer will not need sleep for the next few days. *'''Great Cardio''': For a few days, the wanderer will be able to run much further and faster without becoming tired.
| accordion-6-title = White Side | accordion-6-contents =
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of [[Level 330]], a level which Heavenly Transportation might send one to.|file=Finalwebp.webp|width=240px}} If a wanderer solves the white side of the cube, they will be granted the utmost blessings it can offer. *'''Heavenly Transportation''': The wanderer will noclip into some of the safest Backrooms levels, predominantly Class Habitable levels like [[Level 330]] and [[Level 399]]. *'''Stock Knowledge''': The wanderer will instantly learn all knowledge from the Survival Guide of the cube's green side. *'''Ambrosia''': A full meal of the wanderer's most favored cuisine will manifest in front of them. *'''Ultimate Defense''': An assault or battle rifle with multiple attachments and spare magazines will manifest in front of the wanderer. For as long as they are alive, only the wanderer will be able to use the firearm. *'''Lulling''': All entities will be docile towards the wanderer, unless provoked. *'''Bountiful Harvest''': The wanderer will be blessed with three of the outcomes of the green side. *'''Jacked''': Gradually over two days, the wanderer will exude great strength and a highly muscular physique. *'''Another Go Round''': If the wander ends up in a near-death situation, instantly, all wounds sustained will be healed, they will gain a massive surge of energy, and all nearby entities will become much less aggressive and will run much slower.
}}
==Additional Information== On June 11, 1982, a young man discovered the first '''Lubik's Cube''' in [[Level 477]]'s arcade complex where he attempted to solve one side of the cube for entertainment and suddenly found himself on [[Level 500]]. As he lived to tell the tale of the cube, more accounts surfaced as more people were aware of the cube, at a rate of one cube sighting every four months for the rest of the 1980s. Wanderers would also discover the functions of the cube and how to use it to its full potential, and the '''Lubik's Cube''', thanks to the potential of the green and white solved side, has become a highly sought object in the Backrooms. Wanderers have formulated guides to solve the cube safely, preventing fatal accidents. Many theories and myths have arisen from the '''Lubik's Cube''''s anomalous abilities. Some beliefs range from entities or higher powers controlling the cube to a potential exit back to the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. Your fate in our hands
For which you have no control
As it has been sealed
